active_paradigms.idea: Implement Brzozowski derivative with ascii_dec and match_eps gating
active_paradigms.description: Fill the derive function with the standard Brzozowski rules: EmptySet→EmptySet, EmptyStr→EmptySet, Char c→(if ascii_dec c a then EmptyStr else EmptySet), Union r1 r2→Union (derive a r1) (derive a r2), App r1 r2→Union (App (derive a r1) r2) (if match_eps r1 then derive a r2 else EmptySet), Star r→App (derive a r) (Star r). Then prove the two helper lemmas app_ne and star_ne (about non-empty strings matching App/Star) to support the main correctness theorem. Finally, prove derive_corr by structural induction on re, using the provided equivalences (app_exists, union_disj, empty_matches_eps, char lemmas) and the newly proved helper lemmas. For App, split by match_eps r1 using match_eps_refl to handle the nullable-first-component case. For Char, dispatch with ascii_dec and char lemmas.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Reduce the number of remaining todos and Admitted by completing derive and discharging derive_corr; correctness proof size and branching in App/Star cases.
active_paradigms.cautions: Require Import Coq.Strings.Ascii for ascii_dec; ensure the fixpoint for match_eps is already available and used only as a boolean in derive. Be careful to use reflect lemmas from match_eps_refl to bridge match_eps r1 = true/false to propositional nullable facts. Avoid simplifying App/Union aggressively in derive itself to keep equivalence proofs straightforward; use only union/app constructors as specified.

active_paradigms.idea: Prove derive correctness via inversion on match derivations with app_ne/star_ne
active_paradigms.description: First close the admitted helper lemmas app_ne and star_ne. Then prove a stronger, inversion-driven version of derive_corr: for all re, s, a, a::s =~ re <-> s =~ derive a re, by analyzing the last constructor used in the derivation of a::s =~ re. Proceed by induction on the match derivation (not on re): handle MChar with char_eps_suffix and ascii_dec; handle MApp by splitting with app_ne and app_exists; handle MUnionL/MUnionR with union_disj; handle MStarApp with star_ne. This approach keeps proofs localized to the structure of the given derivation, often reducing the need for complex structural case splits on re and avoiding nested boolean reasoning.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Discharge the two admitted structural lemmas early and streamline derive_corr proof obligations, reducing proof branching and proof script size.
active_paradigms.cautions: In the MApp case, carefully distinguish the empty/non-empty prefix using app_ne; ensure consistent use of associativity of ++ if needed. For Star, repeatedly use star_ne and MStar0 to manage the recursive suffix. Keep inversion steps tidy (remember to subst after inversion) to avoid stale equalities. Where boolean conditions arise (e.g., match_eps r1), use match_eps_refl to bridge booleans to propositions.

tried_paradigms.idea: Brzozowski derivatives with reflection and fold_left
tried_paradigms.description: Implement the matching algorithm via Brzozowski derivatives: define derive a re to compute the derivative of re with respect to character a, and make regex_match s re = match_eps (fold_left derive s re). Prove derive_corr (derivation correctness) by structural induction on re using the standard derivative equations, and use app_ne and star_ne lemmas to handle the cons case a :: s for App and Star. Prove match_eps_refl (already provided) to reflect nullable. Then prove regex_match_correct by induction on s, rewriting with derive_corr at each step and finishing with match_eps_refl. Keep derive purely structural (no simplification needed for correctness) to make proofs straightforward. The key is to set up helper lemmas for App and Star with non-empty inputs, which are already stated; you can use them to bridge the cons case equivalences. Finally, define regex_match using List.fold_left derive over the string and feed the result to match_eps.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Increase qed_count by fully defining derive and regex_match and discharging derive_corr and regex_match_correct; reduce todo_count and admitted_count without adding extra obligations.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Be precise with the derivative of App: der(App r1 r2) = Union (App (der r1) r2) (if match_eps r1 then der r2 else EmptySet). For Star: der(Star r) = App (der r) (Star r). Ensure reflect lemmas convert iff to reflect cleanly. Do not over-simplify regex constructors in code; keep soundness proofs simple first. When proving cons cases, use app_ne and star_ne to avoid manual destructs on concatenations. Keep recursion structural on the regex in derive to satisfy Coq’s termination checker.

tried_paradigms.idea: Compile to ε-NFA and simulate using MSets for finite-state execution
tried_paradigms.description: Translate the regular expression into an ε-NFA (Thompson construction) inside Coq, representing sets of states with finite sets from MSets. Define epsilon-closure and move functions and a simulator that computes the reachable state set after consuming the input list. Prove that the compiler preserves semantics (soundness and completeness) by induction on the regex structure and by simulation invariants on runs vs. execution over sets. Implement regex_match by running the simulator and checking if any accepting state is reached. This approach replaces derivatives with state-set transitions and can be cleaner for unions, concatenations, and stars via standard NFA composition. Use fold_left to iterate transitions over the input string, taking epsilon-closures at each step.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Reduce admitted_count by providing a complete, alternative decision procedure with clean invariants, and eliminate todos by implementing regex_match through NFA simulation.
tried_paradigms.cautions: Choose a concrete finite set implementation (e.g., MSetWeakList over a decidable state type) and prove necessary lemmas about closures and unions. Carefully define epsilon-closure to ensure termination (use a worklist algorithm over finite sets). Keep state identifiers finite and avoid fresh name collisions by threading a counter through the compiler. Proofs require invariants relating NFA paths to regex matches; structure them to avoid rewriting explosions. Imports from Coq.MSets and decidable equality for state types are necessary.
